summary: Round 1 review of commit 204886e. Both files implement Task 4 ACs precisely: CrashReportActivity is a plain ComponentActivity (no @AndroidEntryPoint, no @Inject), uses the non-Hilt LogExportManager(applicationContext) secondary ctor, registers ActivityResultContracts.CreateDocument("text/plain") with a Locale.ROOT timestamped filename, guards double-taps via a mutableStateOf(false)-backed launching field that clears in finally for success / IOException / cancel branches, and follows the required success path (buildExport(CrashReport) → writeTo → delete crash.log → toast → finish()). RestartCrashBanner is a pure stateless Composable with the required Card { Row { Icon, Text, TextButton, IconButton } } shape, Icons.Outlined.Warning/Close, and enables/disables only the save TextButton per launching. No critical or high findings; two low-severity observations on a blocking File.delete() on Dispatchers.Main and a minor DRY duplication of LOGS_DIR / CRASH_LOG constants between this activity and LogExportManager.

findings.title: File.delete() on Dispatchers.Main.immediate after writeTo returns
findings.description: handleSafResult runs under lifecycleScope (Dispatchers.Main.immediate). buildExport and writeTo internally switch to Dispatchers.IO via withContext, but on return the coroutine is back on Main. The subsequent File(filesDir, "logs/crash.log").delete() is a blocking filesystem call executed on the main thread. In practice the file is ≤100 KB and the call is fast, and this path only runs on the one-shot recovery screen, so user-visible impact is negligible — but the pattern deviates from the "no blocking I/O on Main" convention the rest of logexport follows.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the delete in withContext(Dispatchers.IO) (or move the entire success tail — delete + Toast prep + finish — into a single withContext(IO) { } followed by an explicit dispatch back to Main for Toast/finish). Alternatively, keep as-is and annotate with a short comment that the call is intentionally on Main because the file is small and the activity is about to finish() anyway.

findings.title: LOGS_DIR / CRASH_LOG constants duplicated between CrashReportActivity and LogExportManager
findings.description: LogExportManager.kt already defines private const val LOG_DIR = "logs" and CRASH_LOG = "crash.log". CrashReportActivity redeclares the same literals as LOGS_DIR / CRASH_LOG. If the log directory ever moves, two files must stay in sync — and the naming even diverges (LOG_DIR vs LOGS_DIR), which is a latent source of confusion.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract a small internal object (e.g. logexport/LogPaths.kt) exposing const val LOG_DIR and CRASH_LOG (and optionally a helper fun crashLogFile(context: Context): File), and consume it from both LogExportManager and CrashReportActivity. Low priority — it is two short literals — but worth doing before Task 5/7 add a third call site.

findings.title: launching state is reactive Compose state but documented as a plain var in the task spec
findings.description: The task hints describe the guard as "var launching: Boolean в CrashReportActivity" (plain field). The implementation uses `private var launching by mutableStateOf(false)` — effectively Compose state hoisted into the Activity. This is functionally superior (recomposition flips the TextButton enabled flag atomically with the guard write), but it is a deviation from the literal hint and is worth calling out so future readers understand the choice. Not a defect.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 1-line KDoc on the launching field explaining why mutableStateOf is used instead of a plain Boolean (so Compose recomposes CrashReportScreen when the guard flips), or leave as-is — current code is idiomatic. Documentation-only; no code change required.
